book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
title: Session III.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Paschasinus the bishop of Lilybæum, in the province of Silicia, and holding the place of the most holy Leo, archbishop of the Apostolic see of old Rome, said in Latin what being interpreted is as follows: It is well known to this beloved of God synod, that divine letters were sent to the blessed and apostolic pope Leo, inviting him to deign to be present at the holy synod.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But since ancient custom did not sanction this, nor the general necessity of the time seemed to permit it, our littleness in the place of himself he τὰ τῆς ἁγίας συνόδου επέτρεψε, and therefore it is necessary that whatever things are brought into discussion should be examined by our interference (διαλαλιᾶς).

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: [The Latin reads where I have placed the Greek of the ordinary text, thus, “commanded our littleness to preside in his place over this holy council.”]

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Therefore let the book presented by our most beloved-of-God brother, and fellow-bishop Eusebius be received, and read by the beloved of God archdeacon and primicerius of the notaries, Aëtius.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And Aëtius, the archdeacon and primicerius of the notaries, took the book and read as follows.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Wherefore the most holy and blessed Leo, archbishop of the great and elder Rome, through us, and through this present most holy synod together with the thrice blessed and all-glorious Peter the Apostle, who is the rock and foundation of the Catholic Church, and the foundation of the orthodox faith, hath stripped him of the episcopate, and hath alienated from him all hieratic worthiness.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Therefore let this most holy and great synod sentence the before mentioned Dioscorus to the canonical penalties.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And when all the most holy bishops had spoken on the subject, they signed this which follows.
